bluf: Today is Q1 close day and the support queue is under maximum pressure. Close-to-onboarding blockers spiked +125% WoW (4→9) with multiple AEs unable to advance closed deals — the Unique Merchant ID field in DC is disabled for several opportunities, creating a single-point-of-failure at the worst possible moment. Contract and Signature workflow is the highest-volume category at 21 cases (+40% WoW), driven by DocuSign delivery failures, SPA generation errors blocking same-day closes, and approved DC values not matching generated contracts. Approvals volume dropped sharply (-55%, 11→5) — likely because AEs are bypassing the approval workflow under Q1 pressure rather than any genuine systemic improvement; watch for this to resurface in Q2. Stratus access requests continued but product coverage gaps (Terminal, Financial Connections, Direct Payments) remain unresolved heading into Q2 rollout expansion.

kpis.stratus_wow: → Flat · coverage gaps block Q2 rollout

pulse.label: Critical · Q1 Close · +125% Spike
pulse.title: Unique Merchant ID field disabled in DC — blocking multiple deals from advancing at quarter close
pulse.detail: Multiple AEs reporting the Unique Merchant ID field is disabled in their opportunities, preventing onboarding steps from completing. Q1 close day = maximum urgency. Each blocked deal is a Q1 at-risk revenue event. No root cause identified in channel.

pulse.label: Critical · EOQ · +40% WoW
pulse.title: Contract/Signature: 21 cases — DocuSign failures + SPA errors + DC/contract value mismatch
pulse.detail: DocuSign emails not reaching customers (jansaloni). SPA generation error "at least one product must be valid" blocking contract creation (schenay, jsieke). Approved DC values not matching SPA-generated contract rates (kikisimonsen, robgallagher). AEs manually requesting same-day support to close Q1 deals.

pulse.label: High · DC Reliability · steady
pulse.title: DC Save / Reliability: 6 cases — "error: undefined" on save-to-opportunity persists
pulse.detail: "Error: undefined" on save-to-opp is the dominant failure mode (patseibt, mathias ×2, llloret, vbuitron). Pricing data disappearing after save in some cases. Discovery Date required field missing from new opp creation flow blocking intake.

pulse.label: High · Product Gaps · +200% WoW
pulse.title: Product/Geo Gaps: 6 cases — Malaysia Connect, EMEA country gaps, iDeal
pulse.detail: Malaysia Connect greyed out despite Trailhead confirming support (jamesongjx). 20 countries missing from connected account territory (nchall). Billing subscription can't be selected (andreasokmen, ztristan). Faster Payout T+2 Intra-EEA unavailable (sofiatorre).

pulse.label: Watch · Approvals · -55% WoW
pulse.title: Approvals dropped to 5 cases — EOQ bypass pattern, not a fix
pulse.detail: Approval volume dropped sharply but the underlying bugs (wrong threshold levels, auto-approval not firing, N/A state) were not resolved. AEs are likely escalating directly to DealOps or shortcutting under Q1 pressure. Expect volume to return to 10+ in Q2 week 1.

stratus.sentiment_detail: Current cohort has adapted — flat volume (5→5) reflects stabilization, not enthusiasm. AEs who hit product coverage walls reverted to DC and stopped asking. Sentiment will turn negative if Q2 expansion rolls out without Terminal, FC, and Direct Payments. The real test is Q2 week 1.

bugs.label: LPM iDeal shows $0 revenue when selected
bugs.detail: levi confirmed: iDeal can be selected in Stratus but the revenue impact shows as $0, making deal metrics incorrect. AEs submitting proposals with understated ONR.
bugs.quote: iDeal is selectable but shows zero revenue contribution — the deal metrics are wrong. — levi

bugs.label: Access provisioning backlog — no formal intake
bugs.detail: Multiple AEs still waiting on Stratus access. Enterprise Platforms-West cohort and adjacent teams requesting access ahead of Q2 expansion. AEs posting directly in #stratus-support.

friction: Terminal, Financial Connections, Direct Payments still absent — AEs forced back to DC mid-deal for 3+ weeks with no ETA communicated
Q2 cohort expansion without these products will trigger a W1-level adoption spike immediately
No formal access intake path — provisioning delays from ad hoc channel requests
priorities: Ship Terminal & Financial Connections before Q2 cohort expansion — without these, Q2 expansion triggers W1-level spike immediately.
Fix LPM iDeal $0 revenue bug — selectable but broken; AEs submitting proposals with incorrect ONR.
Add Direct Payments support — blocks EMEA/APAC deals from migrating to Stratus.
Create formal access intake process — eliminate ad hoc #stratus-support provisioning requests.
Publish Q2 rollout timeline and product roadmap — AEs asking repeatedly, uncertainty suppresses adoption.

blockers.label: Correct pricing triggering wrong approval level
blockers.detail: ejdunne: GR=1.04, no below floor, still routing to L2. abatuure: deal pricing requesting DC team approval on already-configured deal. Carryovers from W1 — threshold logic not fixed.
blockers.quote: GR is 1.04, No below floor, but still going to Level 2 approval. — ejdunne

blockers.label: Auto-approval not firing despite meeting criteria
blockers.detail: dori: improved GR, ONR, and BPS vs prior deal. Deal Pricing applied manual override. Contract generation still failed. Clone + re-save = same result.
blockers.quote: I have no way forward to generate a contract and close this deal by EOM. — dori

blockers.label: SPA reads different ONR than approved DC — re-loops into approval
blockers.detail: kikisimonsen: received final ONR approval email, then Ironclad generated SPA with different ONR and re-routed through approval ladder. Confirmed systemic.
blockers.quote: Showing my ONR negotiated is different and suggesting to go again into approval ladder — seems a systematic issue. — kikisimonsen

blockers.label: W1 carryover: volume drop reflects EOQ bypass, not resolution
blockers.detail: W1 had 11 approval issues including submit errors, N/A states, Ironclad internal approver blockers. None resolved in W2. AEs escalating to DealOps directly.

cross_functional: Deal Pricing: manual overrides requested but don't fully unblock — dori's deal still failed after override
DealOps absorbing Q1 pressure directly via Jira/DM — approval drop in channel masks real volume
DC Engineering: threshold logic mismatched to documented rules for 3 consecutive runs

exception_breakdown.wrong_level_detail: Deals at correct pricing routed to L2+ unnecessarily
exception_breakdown.auto_approve_detail: Criteria met but manual override still required

priorities: Approval level threshold logic wrong — GR>1.0 routing to L2; 3rd consecutive run; needs DC Engineering audit vs. documented rules.
Auto-approval not firing — even after Deal Pricing override; no fallback path; AEs stranded at Q1 close.
SPA reads different ONR than approved DC — confirmed systemic; re-loops approved deals.
DealOps hidden load from EOQ bypass — need Jira ticket volume data for true picture.
No self-serve troubleshooting path — every incident requires a support interaction.

actions.title: Unblock Unique Merchant ID field today
actions.detail: Multiple AEs cannot complete onboarding steps for closed Q1 deals because the field is disabled. Each blocked deal is a Q1 revenue risk. DC Engineering needs to identify root cause and enable the field immediately or provide a manual workaround.
actions.owner: DC Engineering — Today · P0 · Q1 close

actions.title: Manually unblock contract generation failures
actions.detail: dori (auto-approval override not persisting through contract gen), schenay + jsieke (SPA product validation error), kikisimonsen + robgallagher (DC↔SPA ONR mismatch re-looping approval). Each needs same-day resolution before Q1 books close.
actions.owner: DC Engineering + DealOps — Today · EOQ critical

actions.title: Fix approval level threshold logic — 3rd consecutive run
actions.detail: GR > 1.0 routing to L2, auto-approval not firing despite criteria being met, approval reverting from
Audit actual threshold configuration vs. documented rules and identify the regression.
actions.owner: DC Engineering + Deal Pricing — This week

actions.title: Fix SPA ↔ DC ONR data consistency
actions.detail: kikisimonsen's deal was re-looped into the approval ladder because SPA generated a different rate than DC approved. Confirmed systemic. SPA must read from the approved DC state.
actions.owner: DC Engineering + Ironclad team — This week

actions.title: Stratus: Ship Terminal + Financial Connections before Q2 cohort expansion
actions.detail: Most-requested missing products for 3+ weeks. Q2 rollout expansion without these will immediately trigger a W1-level spike in #stratus-support. Get an ETA and communicate it to the AE population proactively.

watchlist.text: Unique Merchant ID blocker — confirmed disabling multiple Q1 closes today. Needs immediate resolution and a post-mortem on why this field can become disabled.
Contract/Signature (21 cases, +40%) — SPA generation errors and DocuSign failures blocked Q1 EOQ closes. Post-quarter: does volume normalize or do unresolved deals create a Q2 backlog?
Approval volume rebound in Q2 W1 — the -55% drop is an EOQ bypass pattern, not a fix. Expect 10–15 cases in Q2 W1 as AEs return to standard workflows.
Stratus Q2 expansion readiness — next cohort without Terminal, FC, and Direct Payments will produce a W1-level spike (13+ issues). Monitor #stratus-support in Q2 W1.
LPM iDeal $0 revenue bug in Stratus — any AE who submitted an iDeal proposal in W1/W2 may have incorrect ONR in their deal record. Audit needed.
Product/Geo Gaps surge (+200%, 2→6) — Malaysia Connect contradiction vs. Trailhead docs. Documentation vs. product reality audit needed before Q2 new business push.
DC Save "error: undefined" — steady at 5–6 cases/week for multiple runs. Discovery Date required field bug adds new opp creation friction. Triage for common root cause.
DealOps hidden load — approval drop in channel likely masks real volume absorbed by DealOps Jira at EOQ. Get Jira ticket volume data for next run.
